items.title: Matplotlib
items.description: Every Seaborn plot renders through Matplotlib figures and axes; use Matplotlib for low-level customization, subplots, and saving after calling Seaborn.

items.title: Covariance and correlation
items.description: Seaborn heatmaps are the standard way to display Pearson correlation matrices computed from numeric columns— linking color intensity to covariance structure.

items.title: Choosing chart types
items.description: Chart-type guidance tells you when a distribution, scatter, box, pair, or heatmap fits the question; Seaborn implements those statistical chart choices with minimal code.

items.title: ROC and AUC
items.description: While ROC curves are often drawn with specialized functions, Seaborn line plots and distribution plots help compare AUC scores across models and inspect score distributions by class.
